headline: U.S. job growth cools sharply to 57,000 in June
detail: A 57K print is well below normal and unemployment ticked up to 4.2% - that signals real economic softness. For your tech and chip names this is a mixed bag: lower growth fears can weigh on cyclical semis (SMH, SOXL) but softer data could also bring rate cuts closer, which is a mild tailwind for high-multiple tech. Net effect is uncertainty, not a clean catalyst either way.
rec: No trade triggered by this alone. Watch whether SMH holds above its $540 stop on any selloff - if macro fear builds, that's your canary for the chip basket.

headline: Tesla deliveries jump 25%, crushing expectations
detail: Strong Tesla deliveries are a sentiment lift for the EV and broad tech space, and signal consumer demand isn't completely dead despite soft jobs. This modestly supports risk-on tone in QQQ and SPY, which you hold. It does not directly move your chip names but a rising tide helps.
rec: You already hold QQQ and SPY at small gains - no reason to add here just on a Tesla headline. Hold and let your existing stops ($670 and $705) protect you.

headline: Brookfield plans AI data centers in London financial district
detail: More AI infrastructure buildout demand globally is a slow-burn positive for semiconductor names - data centers mean chip demand. This supports the long-term thesis behind SMH and SOXL but is not a same-day trading catalyst.
rec: No immediate trade. If SMH stabilizes above $540 and macro fear fades, it strengthens the hold case, but don't add on this headline alone given your margin exposure.

headline: Jeff Bezos family office backed five AI startups in June
detail: Smart money continuing to pour into AI is a sentiment signal that the AI capex cycle is intact, which underpins demand for the chips and software names you're concentrated in. Again, no immediate price mover but confirms the macro backdrop for your book.
rec: No action needed - this confirms your existing thesis but gives no entry signal and no scanner setup aligns with a new buy today given current market conditions.
